title: [Bug]: Cancelling podman login breaks current console
body:
### Issue Description

In `podman login`, when cancelling the password login, the console is left in a broken state. The character echo remains turned off and the console looks weird (see Screenshot)

![podman-broken](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1527829/216949332-171575c6-3ed1-4131-b523-e9d22fa135a4.png)


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `podman login`
2. Type in any username
3. Cancel the password login via CTRL+C
4. Console is broken

### Describe the results you received

The console is broken - see the above posted screenshot

### Describe the results you expected

Console would work normally

comments.body: I'd like to try to come up with a patch for this issue as well. Please give me some time, as this would be the first time I'm gonna dive into `podman` itself.
@grisu48 Thanks contributions are always welcome, FYI the login code is in c/common: https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/pkg/auth/auth.go

Thanks for the info. I've been digging a bit into this and figured this is a upstream bug in https://github.com/golang/go/issues/31180.

I guess we can close this issue, as this is not podman specific but an issue in x/crypto/ssh/terminal.

Perhaps we could still apply a workaround, as suggested in https://github.com/golang/go/issues/31180#issuecomment-1133854964. Will try if this makes a difference and if it would be save.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@grisu48 any progress on this?  Could we catch the SIGTERM in podman login and then reset the terminal?

Hi @rhatdan, I forgot about this. I'm working on https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1312 to bring it in discussable shape, but had some issues with testing this on Windows. Expect an update on the PR this or the next week and thanks for pinging me.
I think this is resolved or as resolved as it possibly can with @grisu48 PR  to common ... please ask to re-open or open a new issue should it persist.
